_comment: TEMPLATE — copy to mappings/emdb/ and adapt to your XLSX layout. This file lives in the mappings root and is NOT auto-loaded by the MappingRegistry (the registry only scans the pyarchinit/, emdb/, and generic/ subdirectories). It exists as a reference/starting point.
_description: Template for XLSX-based EMdb tables. Same column_mappings schema as the pyarchinit template, but with table_settings.format_type='xlsx' and a sheet name.
name: Template — EMdb-style XLSX sheet
description: Replace this description with one specific to your sheet.

table_settings._comment_format_type: format_type='xlsx' selects MappedXLSXImporter / XLSXImporter on the consumer side.
table_settings._comment_sheet_name: sheet_name is the worksheet to read (string name, or 0-based index).
table_settings._comment_start_row: start_row is 1-indexed and refers to the first row that contains DATA. Row 1 always holds column headers; if start_row=1, data begins on row 2.

Project._comment: FILTER COLUMN. The consumer can call importer.get_filter_columns() and importer.get_distinct_values('Project') to render a dropdown so the user picks one project before import. filter_required=true means the consumer should force a selection.

Phase._comment: Optional secondary filter. Combined with 'Project' via AND.

ID._comment: Row identifier. Becomes the name of the stratigraphic node. Exactly ONE column must be is_id=true.

Description._comment: Free-text description, stored on the StratigraphicNode.description field.

Material._comment: Regular property column → one PropertyNode per row, linked via 'has_property'.
